tips: [Junk Pirates] Fly your Hound backwards to take offensive advantage of the small ballistic mount.
[Junk Pirates] An Aurora should almost always be fielded with a full complement of Anti-Matter Blasters.
[Junk Pirates] ASP Courier fleets might seem a bit squishy, but if you attack one don't expect to get away lightly.
[Junk Pirates] Some things may not seem GOOD ideas, but it doesn't mean they aren't FUN ideas.
[Junk Pirates] You can disable parts of Junk Pirates if, for some EXTRAORDINARY reason parts of it are not to your taste. Check mendoncaModSettings.ini in the mod folder.
[Junk Pirates] Always fly the smallest ship you can; with the biggest guns.
[Junk Pirates] Watch out for the Kite with the Reaper. Your greatest enemy is hubris.
[Junk Pirates] Always remember that the Luddic Path are people too.

[Junk Pirates] You know that funny scraping noise you can hear coming from the back of the ship? It's not your Unreliable Subsystems; it's a rogue Alpha Core remotely reprogramming the docking computers at Jangala.
[Junk Pirates] ASP specific ships are capable at surviving combat scenarios - but they are not generally strong offensively.
[Junk Pirates] Junk Pirates ships are generally all over the place in terms of design and layout; but they can fill most roles.
[Junk Pirates] Most problems can be solved with more Hellbores.

[Junk Pirates] The P.A.C.K. main fleet are equipped with a 'mesh' communications system. It gives bonuses when you deploy mixed fleets, so if you are running a P.A.C.K. fleet remember to mix it up with your hull types!
[Junk Pirates] The P.A.C.K. mesh hullmod gives discrete bonuses when you deploy 5, or even 10 unique hull types in an engagement. Mix it up!
